key_insight: Anisotropic short-range chemical order in wurtzite ScAlN is an independent control knob that raises the intrinsic ferroelectric switching barrier, especially through columnar Sc–N–Al–N–Sc connectivity along the polar c axis.
engineering_relevance: Engineering local chemical order could tune ferroelectric switching barriers in ScAlN devices without changing alloy composition, offering a new pathway for performance optimization.
method: First-principles canonical sampling of wurtzite ScAlN alloys
Compare intrinsic switching barriers for random-alloy versus SRO configurations
Motif-resolved analysis of local Sc–N coordination patterns
evidence_signal: Across a broad composition range, the simulations show robust anisotropic SRO and a systematic increase in switching barrier relative to random-alloy structures, with columnar Sc–N–Al–N–Sc motifs tracking barrier variation.
watch_item: The conclusions are based on idealized first-principles alloy configurations, so real films may differ because of growth history, defects, strain, and interface effects.
paper_title: Anisotropic Short-Range Order Modulates Ferroelectric Switching in Wurtzite ScAlN Alloys
